subject: Geography
title: Underground Rivers
description: A quick, practical guide to Underground Rivers.

hook.text: The Puerto Princesa Underground River flows 8 km through a cave before emptying into the sea.

buildup.text: Underground rivers carve cave systems and create hidden reservoirs of fresh water.

discovery.text: The Yucatán's underground river system may be the world's longest—over 350 km and counting.

twist.text: Pollutants dumped on the surface reach underground rivers in hours through porous limestone.

climax.text: Billions of people depend on groundwater connected to these invisible river systems.

punchline.text: The rivers you can't see are the ones that matter most.

quiz.question: Why are underground rivers vulnerable to pollution?
options: Porous limestone lets surface pollutants reach them quickly
They have no current to flush pollutants
